summary: The session focused on drafting a blog post announcing a 17,000-square-foot retail expansion in Nocatee, Florida, anchored by a high-end steakhouse called Chophouse Nocatee, which features a rooftop bar. Key stakeholders, including Michael O’Steen from The PARC Group and Riley Skinner from Skinner Brothers Realty, expressed excitement about the expansion's potential to enhance the Town Center's walkability and community appeal.
facts: Nocatee, Florida is undergoing a 17,000-square-foot retail expansion in its Town Center, anchored by a high-end steakhouse called Chophouse Nocatee with a rooftop bar. Michael O’Steen is the Managing Director at The PARC Group, Nocatee’s master developer, who expressed excitement about the new additions to the Town Center. Chophouse Nocatee is owned and managed by the same team behind Chophouse Thirteen in Mandarin. Riley Skinner is the Vice President of Skinner Brothers Realty, who stated that the expansion will create a more walkable Town Center. The expansion aims to enhance the community experience by providing unique shops, restaurants, and entertainment options.
